persona.description: A decisive shopper who prioritizes fit and quality over price.

behavior_description: 0-2s: The shopper stops at the display and reaches toward the product. 2-5s: The shopper touches the accessible demo item on the open tray, releases it, then reaches for it again. 5-8s: The shopper repeats the handling motion while studying the item closely with hands visible. 8-10s: The shopper leaves the demo item on the tray and remains undecided.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper stops at the display and reaches toward the product.
The shopper touches the accessible demo item on the open tray, releases it, then reaches for it again.
The shopper repeats the handling motion while studying the item closely with hands visible.
The shopper leaves the demo item on the tray and remains undecided.

kling_prompt_sections.camera: 10-second fixed in-store surveillance-style shot, slightly elevated oblique angle; customer body trajectory, dwell time, hand movement, product display, and counter area visible; face detail is helpful but not required; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
kling_prompt_sections.scene: a quiet watch boutique with a glass display counter; visible product setup: an accessible demo sample on an open presentation tray on top of the counter, outside any glass case; the shopper can naturally touch only this demo sample, while the enclosed display stays in the background.

behavior_timeline.event: The shopper stops at the display and reaches toward the product.
The shopper touches the accessible demo item on the open tray, releases it, then reaches for it again.
The shopper repeats the handling motion while studying the item closely with hands visible.
The shopper leaves the demo item on the tray and remains undecided.

kling_prompt_sections.negative: no extreme overhead shot, no tiny distant customer, no occluded product area, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.

kling_prompt:
Camera: 10-second fixed in-store surveillance-style shot, slightly elevated oblique angle; customer body trajectory, dwell time, hand movement, product display, and counter area visible; face detail is helpful but not required; one primary shopper, natural movement, no subtitles, no voiceover, no brand logos.
Scene: a quiet watch boutique with a glass display counter; visible product setup: an accessible demo sample on an open presentation tray on top of the counter, outside any glass case; the shopper can naturally touch only this demo sample, while the enclosed display stays in the background.
Customer behavior style: The shopper stays near the display and examines details with visible curiosity.
Behavior timeline:
- 0-2s: The shopper stops at the display and reaches toward the product.
- 2-5s: The shopper touches the accessible demo item on the open tray, releases it, then reaches for it again.
- 5-8s: The shopper repeats the handling motion while studying the item closely with hands visible.
- 8-10s: The shopper leaves the demo item on the tray and remains undecided.
Negative constraints: no extreme overhead shot, no tiny distant customer, no occluded product area, no extra shoppers, no completed purchase, no salesperson speech, no exaggerated acting, no floating labels, no hidden-category labels, no intervention labels, no scoring text, no UI overlays.
